name: next-js-better-auth-integration
description: A conceptual skill for integrating Better Auth with Next.js App Router
capabilities: Initialize Better Auth with database adapters and providers
Configure JWT token generation and refresh mechanisms
Wrap Next.js applications with authentication context providers
Implement protected routes using Next.js middleware
Use authentication hooks for session state access in components
author: Claude
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing only conceptual guidance for authentication implementation. All static findings are false positives triggered by security-related keywords in documentation text. No executable code, network calls, or file system access exists in this skill.
valueStatement: Implementing secure authentication in Next.js can be complex with session management, JWT handling, and protected routes. This skill provides a conceptual framework for integrating Better Auth with the App Router architecture for production-ready authentication.
userTitle: Integrate Better Auth with Next.js App Router
limitations: This is a conceptual skill - it provides guidance patterns rather than executable code
Does not include actual authentication provider credentials or database setup
Requires understanding of Next.js server and client components

useCases.title: Add auth to Next.js apps
useCases.description: Implement secure user authentication with session management in Next.js applications using Better Auth

useCases.title: Configure JWT authentication
useCases.description: Set up JWT token generation, validation, refresh mechanisms, and secure storage patterns

useCases.title: Create protected routes
useCases.description: Build middleware-based route protection with proper redirect handling and session verification

promptTemplates.title: Basic auth setup
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I set up Better Auth in my Next.js App Router project with database configuration and JWT tokens?

promptTemplates.title: Session management
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to wrap my Next.js app with Better Auth provider and access session data in both server and client components.

promptTemplates.title: Protected routes
promptTemplates.prompt: Create middleware to protect routes in Next.js App Router, redirecting unauthenticated users to login.

promptTemplates.title: Custom auth flow
promptTemplates.prompt: Design an authentication flow with social login, session persistence, and proper error handling for my Next.js application.

bestPractices: Use HTTP-only secure cookies for JWT token storage to prevent XSS attacks
Implement proper session invalidation and token refresh mechanisms
Validate all authentication inputs and implement rate limiting on auth endpoints
antiPatterns: Storing JWT tokens in localStorage exposes them to XSS attacks
Skipping middleware protection on sensitive routes creates security gaps
Not implementing proper session expiration leads to perpetual access

faq.question: Does this skill work with Next.js Pages Router?
faq.answer: No, this skill is designed specifically for Next.js App Router. Pages Router requires different patterns.

faq.question: What databases does Better Auth support?
faq.answer: Better Auth supports multiple adapters including PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, SQLite, and Drizzle ORM.

faq.question: Can I use this with custom authentication?
faq.answer: Yes, but significant customization may conflict with Better Auth's built-in patterns.

faq.question: How are credentials and secrets handled?
faq.answer: Authentication secrets must be stored securely in environment variables, never in code.

faq.question: Why is authentication failing in production?
faq.answer: Check that cookies are configured for your production domain and HTTPS is enabled for secure cookie storage.

faq.question: How is this different from NextAuth.js?
faq.answer: Better Auth offers a simpler API, built-in type safety, and a plugin architecture for extended functionality.
